description: A composite designed graphic in ICE's recurring 'rogues-gallery' arrest-card style: a booking-style mugshot photo inset (with rounded corners and a thin border) is layered over a faded full-bleed background photograph of a quiet suburban residential street with trees, fences, utility poles and houses under a partly cloudy blue sky (apparently the subject's neighborhood). The inset mugshot shows a young man with dark, voluminous curly hair, a thin mustache and soul patch, looking at the camera against a plain light-gray wall; he wears a dark hooded sweatshirt printed with bowling-pin graphics and the word 'COMMACK.' There is no large headline visible on this particular image, but the layout (mugshot floated over the subject's local setting) matches ICE's enforcement-card design template. Per the tweet, the subject is Fredis Antonio Gonzalez-Recinos, a green-card holder arrested for child sexual abuse. The propaganda technique is to publicize and shame an individual arrestee, presenting immigration enforcement as protecting communities from dangerous 'criminal aliens.' Target audience: the general public and immigration-enforcement supporters.
